California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
February 7, l.99O
Lance Olson
o1son, Connell-y, Hagel, Fong & Leidigh
30O Capitol Ma1I, Suite 350
Sacranento, CA 9 5814
RE: Your Request
Our File No. for Advice
A-90-028
Dear !lr. Olson:
You have requested advice on behalf of the California Dental
Hygienists, Associatj,on political- Action Committee (CAL-HY-PAC)
concerning the campaign provisions of the potitical 
Reform Act.1
OUESTION
Is CAL-HY-PAC required to maintain records on association
members whose annual dues earrnarked for the pAc are less than $25
if the PAC kno\^rs the cumulative total for the calendar year fron
individual members nay exceed 925?
CONCLUSION
There is no requirement for cAL-Hy-pAc to maintain records on
members lrhose annual dues earnarked for the pAc are less than $25
other than the date and total amount of such contributions, even
though the total amount contributed by certain members may exceed
$25 for the calendar year.
Although you indicated that no member contributes g]'oo or
more in a calendar year, if this situation changes please note
that even though the Political 
Reform Act does not require
detailed recordkeeping of any source which contributes less than
925 at a tine, if the committee knows, or has reason to know, that
any one source nay contribute amounts under 925 totaling gloo
during the calendar year, the purposes of the political 
Reforro Act
will be served if detailed records of such contributions are kept
and the total contributions ite:nized on the comrnittee, s campaign
statenent.
1 
co.r"rnment Code Sections 81OOO-91015. A11 statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Cornmission regulations appear at 2 Catifornia Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. AII- references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958tr4-O8O7 . (916) 322-566(.)


Lance Olson
Page fwo
FACTS
cAL-Hy-pAc is the sponsored committee of the California
Dental, Hygienists, Association. The pAc receives its
contributions through a systen where rnernbers of the association
earrnark a specific amount of their annual dues as contiiuuti.on= to
the PAC.
The amount of the annual earmarked. palqnent usually is less
than $25. No individual member's total earmarked contributions
froru annual dues equals or exceeds g1OO.
. 
.. ocg3:ionally, members are solicited for direct contrj.butions
to the PAc in addition to the normal amount earmarked throuqn
their dues payment.
ANALYSI S
Section 84104 requires treasurers of
such detailed accounts, records, bi11s and
necessary to prepare campaign statements..
clarifies that:
committees t。 ::maintain
receipts that are
..::  Regu■ation ■840■
...accounts (are not required to) contain detail as to
contributions received of less tiran g25 ottrer itran-ttrJ aate
and total amount of such contributionsi ...
.__ _.II you have any additional questions, please contact ne at
(916) 322-5662.
Sincerely,
Kathryn
General
litical 
Refono Consultant
